title: High Output Management
author: Andrew S. Grove
subline: A manager is the multiplier of other people's output.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A simple ORGANIZATION CHART: 1 manager at top, 4 directors under, 16 ICs under them. The manager box is RED. Title is below. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 380)" fill="none" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="1.5">
    <!-- Manager (top, centered) — RED box -->
    <rect x="-100" y="0" width="200" height="60" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2.5"/>
    <text x="0" y="38" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.2em">MANAGER</text>
    <!-- 4 directors (level 2) -->
    <g stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="1">
      <rect x="-580" y="220" width="160" height="50"/>
      <text x="-500" y="252" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.2em">DIRECTOR</text>
      <rect x="-260" y="220" width="160" height="50"/>
      <text x="-180" y="252" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.2em">DIRECTOR</text>
      <rect x="100" y="220" width="160" height="50"/>
      <text x="180" y="252" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.2em">DIRECTOR</text>
      <rect x="420" y="220" width="160" height="50"/>
      <text x="500" y="252" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.2em">DIRECTOR</text>
    </g>
    <!-- 16 ICs (level 3) -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1">
      <g>
        <rect x="-660" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="-560" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="-460" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="-360" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="-340" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="-240" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="-140" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="-40" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="120" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="220" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="320" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="420" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="440" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="540" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="640" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
        <rect x="740" y="440" width="80" height="40"/>
      </g>
    </g>
    <g font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="10" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="0.18em">
      <text x="-620" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="-520" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="-420" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="-320" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="-300" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="-200" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="-100" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="0" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="160" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="260" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="360" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="460" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="480" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="580" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="680" y="464">IC</text>
      <text x="780" y="464">IC</text>
    </g>
    <!-- Connecting lines: manager to directors, directors to ICs -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1">
      <line x1="0" y1="60" x2="-500" y2="220"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="60" x2="-180" y2="220"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="60" x2="180" y2="220"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="60" x2="500" y2="220"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="270" x2="-620" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="270" x2="-520" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="270" x2="-420" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="270" x2="-320" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="-180" y1="270" x2="-300" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="-180" y1="270" x2="-200" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="-180" y1="270" x2="-100" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="-180" y1="270" x2="0" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="180" y1="270" x2="160" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="180" y1="270" x2="260" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="180" y1="270" x2="360" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="180" y1="270" x2="460" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="500" y1="270" x2="480" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="500" y1="270" x2="580" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="500" y1="270" x2="680" y2="440"/>
      <line x1="500" y1="270" x2="780" y2="440"/>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: Grove's central thesis: a manager's job is to multiply the output of everyone under them. The cover is a literal org chart — 1 manager (RED box, 2.5px stroke), 4 directors, 16 ICs — drawn in hairlines with connecting lines. The manager is the only red element. The chart IS the argument. Title sits in the bottom 1/3, weight 500 command, mixed-case. Brand 'MANAGER ×' in the top-left (the multiplication); pill 'OKR' at the top — Grove's OKR system, born from this book.
